mnemonics.title: Virtual Function Purpose
mnemonics.content: V.I.R.T.U.A.L - Vtable Implementation Reveals True Underlying Adaptive Linkage; Virtual functions enable Runtime polymorphism through vtable Implementation Reveals True function at Underlying Adaptive Linkage (runtime)

mnemonics.title: Abstract Class Rules
mnemonics.content: A.B.S.T.R.A.C.T - Abstract Base Shouldn't Totally Realize Any Concrete Type; An abstract class cannot be instantiated directly

mnemonics.title: Polymorphism Requirements
mnemonics.content: I.P.P - Inheritance needed, Pointer/Reference required, Pure virtual function declared

acronyms.usage: Lookup table storing function pointers for virtual functions, one per class with virtual functions
Hidden pointer in each object pointing to class vtable for runtime function resolution
Idiom to hide implementation details, often used with polymorphism
Mechanism to determine type of object at runtime, used by dynamic_cast

visualTips.visualization: Imagine each class with virtual functions has a dispatch table (vtable) hanging on the wall. Each object of that class has a small tag (vptr) pointing to its class's table. When calling a virtual function, the program follows the tag to find which function to call.
Draw a flowchart: Base Pointer → Object (could be Base or Derived) → vptr → vtable → Virtual Function Address → Execute correct function. The arrow changes based on actual object type.
Think of a pizza (derived object) being cut and only the crust (base part) being taken. The toppings (derived data) are 'sliced off' and lost.
Draw an abstract class as a blueprint with 'TODO' notes (=0) on some methods. Derived classes must fill in these TODO items to become concrete and usable.

analogies.concept: Base Class Pointer to Derived Object
analogies.analogy: Like a TV remote (base pointer) that can control different TV models (derived objects). The same remote button (virtual function) triggers different actions depending on which TV is connected.
Like a restaurant menu (base class) with a 'dessert' option. The actual dessert served depends on which restaurant (derived class) you order from, decided when you place your order (at runtime).
Like a phonebook (vtable) that maps names (function names) to phone numbers (function addresses). Each class has its own phonebook updated with the correct numbers.
Like a job posting (abstract class) that defines required skills (=0, pure virtual) but doesn't hire anyone directly. Candidates (derived classes) must meet the requirements to get the job.
Like having a master key (virtual destructor) that can open any lock (derived class destructor). Without it, you can only open the outer lock (base destructor) and leave things inside unlocked.
